title: MetricsEndpointDiscovery docstring usage example is invalid Python (broken import + wrong class name)
summary: The module-level usage example in lib/charms/observability_libs/v0/metrics_endpoint_discovery.py (docstring, lines 19-39) cannot be copy-pasted and run. Two defects: (1) the import statement on lines 22-25 is `from charms.observability_libs.v0.metrics_endpoint_discovery import` immediately followed by `MetricsEndpointCharmEvents,` with NO opening parenthesis, yet line 25 closes with `)` -> SyntaxError: unmatched ')'. (2) The example imports a class `MetricsEndpointCharmEvents` (line 23) that does not exist; the real class is `MetricsEndpointChangeCharmEvents` (defined line 101), which is what the example body itself actually uses on line 29 (`on = MetricsEndpointChangeCharmEvents()`). So the import list is both syntactically broken and names a non-existent symbol.
why_good_first_issue: Self-contained, no domain knowledge required: fix a few lines inside one docstring so the example parses and imports the correct, existing class. Verifiable in seconds with a one-line `compile()` check. No tests or runtime behaviour change. This is the canonical 'getting started' example developers copy when adopting the library, so the fix has real user value. Clear definition of done: example compiles and imports names that exist in the module.
repro_steps: On VM cos: multipass exec cos -- git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/observability-libs ~/work/observability-libs; then `grep -n '' lib/charms/observability_libs/v0/metrics_endpoint_discovery.py | sed -n '19,40p'` to view the example, and `grep -n '^class ' lib/.../metrics_endpoint_discovery.py` to confirm the real class names. Reproduced the SyntaxError by extracting lines 20-25 of the docstring and running python3 compile() on them.
repro_result: confirmed: extracting the example import block and running compile() yields `SyntaxError: unmatched ')' at line 6`. Source inspection: line 22 `from ...metrics_endpoint_discovery import` (no '('), line 23 `MetricsEndpointCharmEvents,`, line 25 `)`. The real classes are MetricsEndpointChangeEvent (l.75), MetricsEndpointChangeCharmEvents (l.101), MetricsEndpointObserver (l.110) -- so `MetricsEndpointCharmEvents` is not defined anywhere. Baseline: `tox -e unit` = 57 passed, 67% cov, ~12s (so no existing failing test covers this docstring).
fix_hint: Replace lines 22-25 with a valid parenthesised import and the correct name, e.g.: `from charms.observability_libs.v0.metrics_endpoint_discovery import (` then `    MetricsEndpointChangeCharmEvents,` then `    MetricsEndpointObserver,` then `)`. Optionally also add the missing `from ops.charm import CharmBase` / `from ops.model import ActiveStatus` imports used by the example body for completeness. Bump LIBPATCH (currently 7, line 65) per the repo's version-bump static check in tox.ini.
notes: Only 2 open issues exist in this repo (#132 'deprecate and archive' and #128 a complex juju resource-patch bug) and neither is beginner-friendly, so both picks are self-identified from code. NOTE the repo is proposed for deprecation/archival in issue #132 (Status: Triage, not yet actioned) -- worth flagging to a newcomer, but the docs fix is still valid against current main. The static env enforces a LIBPATCH bump on any changed lib file (tox.ini [testenv:static]).

title: Remove dead [tool.black] config and misleading 'using black' comment from pyproject.toml (project uses ruff)
summary: pyproject.toml carries a `[tool.black]` section (lines 44-47: line-length=99, target-version=['py38']) and a comment on line 57 `# Ignore E501 because using black creates errors with this`, but black is NOT a dependency of the project and is not invoked anywhere. The dev extra (lines 16-35) lists ruff/codespell/pyright but no black, uv.lock contains no black, and tox.ini runs ruff for both linting ([testenv:lint] `ruff check`) and formatting ([testenv:fmt] `ruff check --fix-only`). The stale black config is dead configuration and the line-57 comment misattributes the E501 ignore to black instead of ruff, which is confusing for contributors.
why_good_first_issue: Pure config cleanup in a single file, no code or test changes, trivially reversible. Easy to verify the claim (grep for 'black' shows only these two lines plus unrelated pip vendored AUTHORS.txt). Good first PR: teaches the contributor where the project's real tooling lives (ruff) while removing misleading cruft. Clear done: black no longer referenced; comment updated to say ruff (or removed); lint/static/unit still green.
repro_steps: On VM cos: clone repo, then `grep -rin black . --include='*.toml' --include='*.ini' --include='*.lock' --include='*.txt'` and `grep -n '' pyproject.toml | sed -n '44,60p'`. Cross-check tooling: `cat tox.ini` (lint/fmt envs both use ruff) and `grep -in black uv.lock` (no match).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: repo-wide grep for 'black' returns ONLY pyproject.toml line 45 ([tool.black]) and line 57 (the comment); the only other hits are .tox/.../pip-26.1.2.dist-info/licenses/AUTHORS.txt (vendored, unrelated names). No black in dependencies, dev extra, or uv.lock. tox.ini [testenv:lint] = `ruff check`, [testenv:fmt] = `ruff check --fix-only` -> ruff is the sole formatter/linter. Baseline `tox -e unit` 57 passed.
fix_hint: Delete the `[tool.black]` block (pyproject.toml lines 44-47) and rewrite the line-57 comment to reference ruff, e.g. `# Ignore E501 because formatting can exceed the line length`, or drop the comment entirely. Run `tox -e lint static unit` to confirm everything still passes (it should -- nothing consumes [tool.black]).
notes: Bundles naturally with a small related tidy if desired: tox.ini also contains a large block of commented-out legacy [testenv:unit]/[testenv:integration]/[testenv:scenario] config (~50 dead lines at the bottom) that a contributor could remove in the same cleanup PR. As with row 1, note the repo is proposed for archival in issue #132.
